mission.title: Install and Configure LiteLLM
mission.description: LiteLLM is a Python SDK and Proxy Server designed to facilitate interactions with over 100 LLM APIs in a unified format. It is ideal for applications requiring cost tracking, load balancing, and logging of AI model requests. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration and potential costs associated with API usage.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the necessary command-line tools to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/v1.30.0/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl

chmod +x ./kubectl

sudo mv ./kubectl /usr/local/bin/kubectl

kubectl version --client

```

> Download kubectl binary
> Make the binary executable
> Move the binary to your PATH
> Verify kubectl installation

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create litellm-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30.0

```

> Create a GKE cluster

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local environment to use the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> Update kubeconfig to use the new cluster

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster

steps.title: Install LiteLLM
steps.description:
Deploy the LiteLLM application to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add litellm https://berriai.github.io/litellm

helm install litellm litellm/litellm --version 1.82.dev1 --namespace litellm --create-namespace

```

> Add the LiteLLM Helm repository
> Install LiteLLM using Helm

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for the LiteLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment litellm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace litellm

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up horizontal pod autoscaling
> Apply monitoring configuration (ensure you have this file ready)

uninstall.title: Uninstall LiteLLM
uninstall.description:
Remove the LiteLLM deployment from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources.

```bash

helm uninstall litellm --namespace litellm

gcloud container clusters delete litellm-cluster --zone=us-central1-a --quiet

```

> Uninstall the LiteLLM Helm release
> Delete the GKE cluster and all orphaned resources

upgrade.title: Upgrade LiteLLM
upgrade.description:
Ensure you back up your configurations, drain the nodes if necessary, and then upgrade the LiteLLM installation.

```bash

kubectl get all --namespace litellm > backup-litelm.txt

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade litellm litellm/litellm --version 1.82.dev1 --namespace litellm

kubectl get pods --namespace litellm

```

> Backup current resources
> Drain the node before upgrading
> Upgrade LiteLLM to the latest version
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to network issues or resource constraints in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check your cluster's resource usage and network settings.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified container image could not be found or accessed.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and ensure your cluster has access to the image repository.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This typically means that the application inside the pod is failing to start.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust the resource requests/limits in your deployment.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new LLM APIs and improved logging features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' when running 'kubectl get pods --namespace litellm'. All pods should be in the 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
